ddls:
CREATE TABLE "incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "event" TEXT /* Title or name of the incident event */,
  "train_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the train involved in the incident */,
  "incident_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident occurred */,
  "location" INTEGER /* Location of the incident */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the incident */,
  "deaths" INTEGER /* Number of deaths resulting from the incident */,
  "injuries" INTEGER /* Number of injuries resulting from the incident */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the incident (e.g., minor, major, catastrophic) */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of the incident (e.g., derailment, collision, mechanical failure) */,
  "weather_conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions at the time of the incident */,
  "time_of_incident" TEXT /* Time when the incident occurred */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* Who reported the incident (e.g., conductor, passenger, witness) */,
  "coordinates_latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the incident location */,
  "coordinates_longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the incident location */,
  "external_data_source_id" TEXT /* ID of the external data source for additional context */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_train_id FOREIGN KEY ("train_id") REFERENCES trains ("train_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incidents_location FOREIGN KEY ("location") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "trains" (
  "train_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each train */,
  "train_name" TEXT /* Name of the train involved in an incident */,
  "train_type" TEXT /* Type of the train (e.g., passenger, freight) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the train */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the train */,
  "year_of_manufacture" INTEGER /* Year the train was manufactured */,
  "operational_status" TEXT /* Current operational status of the train (e.g., active, retired, under maintenance) */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance check */,
  "operator" TEXT /* Railway company operating the train */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("train_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the incident occurred */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the incident occurred */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the incident occurred */,
  "coordinates_latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the location */,
  "coordinates_longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the location */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  "nearest_railway_station" TEXT /* Nearest railway station to the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "incident_analysis" (
  "analysis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analysis record */,
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* ID of the incident being analyzed */,
  "cause" TEXT /* Identified cause or contributing factors of the incident */,
  "recommendations" TEXT /* Recommendations for safety improvements based on the analysis */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date when the analysis was conducted */,
  "financial_impact" TEXT /* Financial impact of the incident */,
  "investigator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the investigator who conducted the analysis */,
  "conclusion" TEXT /* Summary conclusion of the analysis */,
  "follow_up_actions" TEXT /* Follow-up actions taken as a result of the analysis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analysis_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_analysis_investigator_id FOREIGN KEY ("investigator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_incident_analysis_incident_id FOREIGN KEY ("incident_id") REFERENCES incidents ("incident_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login purposes */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., investigator, admin, analyst) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for secure login */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user's account (e.g., active, suspended, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
